LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS VINAY PATEL AND RITA PATEL'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. (CCP 425.16.) DEFENDANTS VINAY PATEL AND RITA PATEL (COLLECTIVELY "DEFENDANTS') HAVE NOT MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF TO SHOW THAT THEY ENGAGED IN PROTECTED ACTIVITY. "FILING A FALSE CRIMINAL COMPLAINT IS AN ILLEGAL ACTIVITY, NOT A CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED EXERCISE OF THE RIGHTS OF PETITION OR FREE SPEECH, AND THAT IS THE END OF [THE DEFENDANT'S] ANTI-SLAPP MOTION." (LEFEBVRE V. LEFEBVRE (2011) 199 CAL.APP.4TH 696, 706.) DEFENDANTS DO NOT PROFFER ANY EVIDENCE TO CONTEST THE ALLEGATION THAT THEY SUBMITTED AN ILLEGAL, FALSE POLICE REPORT. "WHEN A DEFENDANT DOES NOT SHOW THAT A "PROTECTED ACTIVITY" UNDERPINS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS, IT IS IRRELEVANT WHETHER THE PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A "PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING" ON HIS OR HER CLAIMS." (GERBOSI V. GAIMS, WEIL, WEST & EPSTEIN, LLP (2011) 193 CAL.APP.4TH 435, 445.) ACCORDINGLY, THIS MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16(C). PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR # 5963

Notice Of Motion For Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party On Renewed Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-29-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS PRAVIN PATEL AND GOVINDJI PATEL'S Motion For Fees And Costs As Prevailing Party On Renewed Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint is DENIED. The Court finds Defendant's motion for reconsideration was submitted in a timely manner and that Defendant complied in good faith with CCP Sec. 425.16(c) as well as CCP Sec. 128.7. See CCP Sec. 128.7 every pleading, petition, written notice of motion, or other similar paper must meet the following: (1) It is not being presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation. (2) The claims, defenses, and other legal contentions therein are warranted by existing law or by a nonfrivolous argument for the extension, modification, or reversal of existing law or the establishment of new law. (3) The allegations and other factual contentions have evidentiary support or, if specifically so identified, are likely to have evidentiary support after a reasonable opportunity for further investigation or discovery. (4) The denials of factual contentions are warranted on the evidence or, if specifically so identified, are reasonably based on a lack of information or belief. Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.

Atty. LAURA S. FLYNN of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel No. (415) 981-6630 appearing for Defendants Vinay Patel and Rita Patel.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS PRAVIN PATEL AND GOVINDJI PATEL'S Motion For Fees And Costs Pursuant To Order Denying Motion To Strike First Amended Complaint is granted. The Plaintiffs are awarded attorneys' fees and costs in their favor in the amount of $20,264.00. Contrary to Defendants' assertions, the Motion is not untimely. Plaintiffs raised the attorney fee issue while litigating the anti-SLAPP motion, not pursuant to this Motion, which seeks the determination of the amount of an award previously ordered by this Court. Thus, the time limits of Cal. Rule of Court 3.1702(b) are inapplicable. Further, the Court's Order denying the anti-SLAPP and awarding attorneys' fees and costs was sufficiently supported by findings of fact and citation to law. Finally, the amount requested by Plaintiffs was not reasonable to the extent that Plaintiffs sought fees for the preparation of a reply on this motion that was never filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling On Anti-Slapp Motion And/Or Renewal Of Anti-Slapp Motion
Off Calendar Jan-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. SHEILA GROPPER NELSON of REsolution Law Firm, P.C., Tel No. (415) 362-2221 appearing for Plaintiffs;
Atty. LAURA S. FLYNN of Low, Ball & Lynch, Tel No. (415) 982-1634 appearing for Defendants Vinay Patel and Rita Patel.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS VINAY PATEL AND RITA PATEL'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling On Anti-Slapp Motion And/Or Renewal Of Anti-Slapp Motion, the Court granted Defendants' Motion for Reconsideration and, upon reconsideration, denied Defendants' Anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike. Defendants have failed to establish that the activity from which Plaintiffs' challenged causes of action arise is protected activity. Defendants contended in the initial motion and again here that the activity was protected because it was activity connected with "legally authorized official proceeding[s]," and that the newspaper announcements were part of an investigation "undertaken in anticipation of civil litigation." (Defendants' Memo. Points & Authorities at 8:8-12.) However, CCP 425.16 "clearly limits the petitioning or free speech activity [that is protected] to that made pursuant to rights granted by the United States or California Constitutions, and neither Constitution grants a United States citizen the right to petition a foreign government." (Guessous v. Chrome Hearts LLC (2009) 179 Cal.App.4th 1177, 1185.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHEILA GROPPER NELSON, TEL. NO. 362-2221 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. LAURA S. FLYNN OF THE LOW BALL & LYNCH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 981-6630 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS VINAY PATEL and RITA PATEL's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. (CCP 425.16.) Defendants Vinay Patel and Rita Patel (collectively "Defendants') have not met their initial burden of proof to show that they engaged in protected activity. "Filing a false criminal complaint is an illegal activity, not a constitutionally protected exercise of the rights of petition or free speech, and that is the end of [the defendant's] anti-SLAPP motion." (Lefebvre v. Lefebvre (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 696, 706.) Defendants do not proffer any evidence to contest the allegation that they submitted an illegal, false police report. "When a defendant does not show that a "protected activity" underpins plaintiff's claims, it is irrelevant whether the plaintiff has shown a "probability of prevailing" on his or her claims." (Gerbosi v. Gaims, Weil, West & Epstein, LLP (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 435, 445.) Accordingly, this motion is frivolous pursuant to CCP 425.16(c). Plaintiffs are entitled to monetary sanctions against Defendants. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR # 5963 =302/ehg
